name: Jordanian Ful with Olive Oil

ingredients.note: cup, chopped, as a parsley stand-in

steps: Warm the beans with a splash of their liquid over medium-low heat, 5-6 minutes.
Mash lightly, leaving some texture, and stir in the lime juice and garlic.
Top with the diced tomatoes and cilantro, and drizzle generously with olive oil.
Ful is a fixture of the Jordanian breakfast table, eaten with warm khubz bread for scooping.
steps_detailed: Warm the beans with a splash of their liquid over medium-low heat, 5-6 minutes.
Mash lightly, leaving some texture, and stir in the lime juice and garlic.
Top with the diced tomatoes and cilantro, and drizzle generously with olive oil.
Ful is a fixture of the Jordanian breakfast table, eaten with warm khubz bread for scooping.
Real fresh parsley is used here now rather than cilantro standing in for it - the two herbs taste meaningfully different, and this dish specifically calls for parsley's flavor.
description: Ful medames, mashed fava beans with olive oil, a Levantine breakfast staple.

name: Jordanian Labneh Bil Zeit

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain
ingredients.note: strained overnight, as a labneh stand-in

steps: Strain the yogurt overnight to thicken it into labneh.
Spread onto a plate, drizzle with the oil, and sprinkle with za'atar.
Serve with warm bread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain
ingredients_full.note: strained overnight, as a labneh stand-in

steps_detailed: Strain the yogurt in a cheesecloth-lined sieve overnight in the fridge until thick and spreadable.
Spread onto a plate, drizzle generously with the oil, and sprinkle with za'atar.
Serve with olives and warm bread - labneh with olive oil is one of the most common Jordanian breakfasts.
description: Labneh with olive oil, a simple, common Levantine breakfast.

name: Jordanian Eggs with Sumac Onions
steps: Saute the onion in the oil until softened and lightly golden, about 6-8 minutes.
Toss with the sumac.
Fry the eggs separately and serve together with warm bread.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion in the oil over medium heat, 8-10 minutes, until softened and lightly golden.
Toss with the sumac off the heat, for its distinctive tangy, lemony flavor.
Fry the eggs in the same pan to your liking, and serve together with warm bread - a simple, common Jordanian breakfast.
description: A simple egg dish, sumac's tartness a defining Levantine flavor.

name: Jordanian Maqluba
steps: Fry the eggplant, brown the chicken with the onion.
Layer eggplant, chicken, then rice in a pot with 1.5 cups water, cover, and cook 20-22 minutes. Flip onto a platter to serve.
steps_detailed: Fry the eggplant and potato slices in the oil until golden, a few minutes per side; set aside on paper towels.

Brown the chicken with the onion, turmeric, cardamom, and cinnamon, 8-10 minutes, then add 1.75 cups water and simmer 15-18 minutes until the chicken is cooked through; lift out the chicken, reserving the spiced broth.
Layer a pot: fried eggplant and potato on the bottom, chicken next, then the raw rice spread evenly on top.
Pour the reserved broth over gently, cover, and cook over low heat 20-22 minutes until the rice is tender.
Rest 5 minutes off the heat, then place a large plate over the pot and flip the whole thing in one motion so the vegetables that were on the bottom end up on top.
Scatter with the pistachios.

Maqluba means 'upside-down' - the flip at the end, revealing the caramelized vegetable layer that was hidden at the bottom, is the entire point of the dish and the reason it's assembled in reverse order in the first place.
description: Maqluba means 'upside-down' - the flip at the end, revealing the caramelized vegetable layer hidden at the bottom, is the entire point of the dish.

name: Jordanian Fattet Hummus

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Warm the chickpeas.
Mix the yogurt with the garlic.
Layer the toasted bread pieces, warm chickpeas, and yogurt in a bowl.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Toast pieces of bread until crisp and golden, then break into small pieces and layer in a wide, shallow bowl.
Warm the chickpeas with a splash of their liquid.
Mix the yogurt with the garlic and a pinch of salt.
Layer the warm chickpeas over the bread, top with the garlic yogurt, drizzle with the oil, and scatter with pistachios - fatteh, layered bread-and-yogurt dishes, are a beloved Jordanian comfort food.
description: Fattet hummus, layered bread, chickpeas, and yogurt, a common Levantine breakfast or light meal.

name: Jordanian Freekeh Soup
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion, garlic, and cinnamon over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the bulgur (standing in for freekeh, smoky roasted green wheat) and chicken broth.
Simmer 22-28 minutes until the grain is tender and the soup has thickened slightly.
A warming, smoky Jordanian soup, especially popular in cooler months.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bob's Red Mill Organic Whole Grain Cracked Freekeh

steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the onion, garlic, and cinnamon over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the bulgur (standing in for freekeh, smoky roasted green wheat) and chicken broth.
Simmer 22-28 minutes until the grain is tender and the soup has thickened slightly.
A warming, smoky Jordanian soup, especially popular in cooler months.
Real freekeh - roasted young green wheat with a distinctive smoky flavor - is used here now instead of plain bulgur, which doesn't have that same roasted character.
description: Freekeh, roasted young green wheat with a distinctive smoky flavor, used here in soup form.

name: Jordanian Shish Barak

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Make a simple dough from the flour and water, and cut into small rounds.
Brown the beef and fill each round, pinching closed into small dumplings.
Simmer the dumplings in the yogurt (thinned with a little water and the garlic) for 12-15 minutes.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Make a simple, firm dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water, and roll out thin, cutting into small rounds.
Brown the beef with the onion, then fill each round with a small spoonful and pinch closed into little dumplings.
Boil the dumplings briefly until they float, then transfer to a pot of the yogurt (thinned with a splash of water and simmered gently with the garlic so it doesn't split) and simmer 12-18 minutes.
Top with pistachios and serve - shish barak, meat dumplings in warm yogurt sauce, is a treasured Jordanian winter comfort food.
description: Shish barak, small meat dumplings served in yogurt sauce, a Levantine specialty.

name: Jordanian Mansaf
steps: Brown the lamb, then simmer in water until tender.
Stir in the yogurt gently over low heat.
Serve over the cooked rice.

ingredients_full.note: formed into a few large chunks
or a fermented dried yogurt (jameed) if available, thinned with water

steps_detailed: Brown the lamb in the oil, 9-11 minutes.
Add 1.5 cups water and simmer 30-35 minutes until tender.
Whisk the yogurt smooth and warm it gently in a separate pot over low heat, stirring constantly so it doesn't split, until it thickens slightly.
Stir a ladle of the lamb's cooking liquid into the yogurt to temper it, then combine everything and simmer gently 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati with the turmeric in 1.1 cups water, covered, 15-18 minutes.
Spread the rice on a platter, pour the lamb and yogurt sauce over, and scatter with the toasted almonds.

Mansaf, Jordan's national dish, is traditionally built on jameed - dried, fermented yogurt reconstituted into a tangy sauce - which isn't a distinct product in this database; fresh yogurt gives a milder, less sour result than the real thing, but the layered rice-lamb-yogurt structure and cardamom are otherwise unchanged.
description: Mansaf, Jordan's national dish, is traditionally built on jameed, dried fermented yogurt reconstituted into a tangy sauce - jameed isn't a distinct product here, so fresh yogurt gives a milder result than the real thing.

name: Jordanian Zarb-Style Roasted Chicken
steps: Season the chicken with the turmeric and garlic.
Roast at 425F with the onion for 30-35 minutes until well browned and cooked through.
Serve with rice.
steps_detailed: Season the chicken generously with the turmeric, garlic, cinnamon, and oil, and let marinate at least 20 minutes.
Roast at 425F (220C) with the onion chunks for 32-38 minutes until deeply browned and cooked through.
Serve with rice - this echoes zarb, a traditional Bedouin method of slow-roasting meat in an underground sand oven in Jordan's desert regions like Wadi Rum.
description: Zarb, meat traditionally cooked in an underground sand oven in the Wadi Rum desert, approximated here with a home roast.

name: Jordanian Beef and Freekeh Stew

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef with the onion, garlic, and cinnamon over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the bulgur and beef broth.
Cover and simmer over low heat 1-1.5 hours until the beef is completely tender and the grain is soft.
Serve hot - a hearty, smoky Jordanian stew.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw
Bob's Red Mill Organic Whole Grain Cracked Freekeh

steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, garlic, and cinnamon over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the bulgur and beef broth.
Cover and simmer over low heat 1-1.5 hours until the beef is completely tender and the grain is soft.
Serve hot - a hearty, smoky Jordanian stew.
Real freekeh - roasted young green wheat with a distinctive smoky flavor - is used here now instead of plain bulgur, which doesn't have that same roasted character.
description: Freekeh, roasted young green wheat, used here in a hearty beef stew.

name: Jordanian Kofta Bil Sanieh (Baked Kofta)

ingredients.note: cup, chopped, as a parsley stand-in

steps: Mix the beef thoroughly with the grated onion, garlic, and herbs, and press into an even layer in a baking dish.
Layer the sliced potato over the meat, then top with the diced tomatoes.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 32-40 minutes until the beef is cooked through and the potato is tender and lightly browned.
A comforting, simple baked Jordanian family dinner.
steps_detailed: Mix the beef thoroughly with the grated onion, garlic, and herbs, and press into an even layer in a baking dish.
Layer the sliced potato over the meat, then top with the diced tomatoes.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 32-40 minutes until the beef is cooked through and the potato is tender and lightly browned.
A comforting, simple baked Jordanian family dinner.
Real fresh parsley is used here now rather than cilantro standing in for it - the two herbs taste meaningfully different, and this dish specifically calls for parsley's flavor.
description: Kofta bil sanieh, spiced ground meat baked with potato and tomato, a Levantine home-cooking staple.

name: Jordanian Hummus

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, and garlic with a splash of water until smooth.
Drizzle with olive oil and serve with warm bread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, garlic, and oil with a splash of water until very smooth.
Spread onto a plate, drizzle with more oil, and serve with warm bread - hummus is central to Jordanian mezze spreads.
description: A simple hummus, a Levantine staple.

name: Jordanian Falafel

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

ingredients.note: cup, chopped, as a parsley stand-in

steps: Blend the chickpeas, garlic, herbs, flour, and turmeric in a food processor into a coarse, moldable paste.
Shape into small balls or patties.
Fry in the oil over medium heat, 3-5 minutes per side, until deeply golden and crisp - falafel is a beloved Jordanian street food staple.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, garlic, herbs, flour, and turmeric in a food processor into a coarse, moldable paste.
Shape into small balls or patties.
Fry in the oil over medium heat, 3-5 minutes per side, until deeply golden and crisp - falafel is a beloved Jordanian street food staple.
Real fresh parsley is used here now rather than cilantro standing in for it - the two herbs taste meaningfully different, and this dish specifically calls for parsley's flavor.
description: Falafel, fried chickpea patties, a Levantine street food staple.

name: Jordanian Kunafa Cheese Bites
steps: Layer the phyllo with the melted butter, fill with the feta, and fold into small packets.
Bake at 375F for 15-18 minutes until golden.
Drizzle with honey before serving.
steps_detailed: Layer sheets of phyllo, brushing each with melted butter, then fill with the crumbled feta and fold into small packets, sealing well.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 16-20 minutes until deeply golden and crisp.
Drizzle with honey before serving - a simplified nod to kunafa, a beloved sweet-savory Levantine treat with especially strong ties to Jordan and Palestine.
description: A bite-sized take on knafeh, phyllo standing in for kataifi shredded phyllo.

name: Jordanian Fried Cauliflower

ingredients.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Fry the cabbage chunks in the oil until deeply golden, about 6-8 minutes.
Whisk the tahini with a splash of water and lime juice for a dipping sauce.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Fry the cabbage chunks (standing in for cauliflower florets) in the oil over medium heat, 8-10 minutes, until deeply golden and crisp at the edges.
Whisk the tahini with the lime juice, garlic, and a splash of water into a smooth dipping sauce.
Serve hot with the sauce alongside - fried cauliflower with tahini is a very popular Jordanian mezze dish.
description: Fried cauliflower, a common Levantine side dish.

name: Jordanian Knafeh
steps: Toss the shredded phyllo with the melted butter.
Press half into a small pan, top with the feta, then the remaining phyllo.
Bake at 375F for 20-25 minutes until golden, then drizzle with the honey.
steps_detailed: Toss the shredded phyllo thoroughly with the melted butter until evenly coated.
Press half into a small buttered pan, top with the crumbled feta, then cover with the remaining phyllo, pressing down firmly.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 22-28 minutes until deeply golden and crisp on top.
Drizzle generously with the honey and scatter with pistachios while still warm - knafeh is one of the most beloved desserts across Jordan and the wider Levant.
description: Knafeh, a beloved Levantine cheese pastry - real knafeh uses kataifi, finely shredded phyllo, and a specific stretchy cheese like akkawi or nabulsi; shredded phyllo and feta stand in here.

name: Jordanian Date Cookies (Maamoul)
steps: Mix the flour, butter, and sugar into a soft, crumbly dough.
Flatten portions, fill with the mashed dates, and reshape into small rounds.
Bake at 350F for 18-22 minutes until just set.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, butter, and sugar into a soft, slightly crumbly dough.
Flatten a portion, fill with the mashed dates, and reshape around it, sealing completely.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 18-24 minutes until just set and very pale - a beloved Jordanian holiday cookie.
description: Ma'amoul, a filled shortbread-style cookie, traditionally stuffed with dates, a beloved dish across the Levant.

name: Jordanian Halawa

ingredients.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Warm the sugar with a splash of water until it forms a thick syrup.
Stir the syrup into the tahini until it thickens and becomes fudge-like.
Fold in the pistachios, press into a small dish, and chill before slicing.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Warm the sugar with a splash of water over medium heat until it dissolves and forms a thick, slightly sticky syrup, about 3-4 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in the tahini and vanilla vigorously until the mixture thickens and turns pale and fudge-like - it will seize up quickly.
Fold in the chopped pistachios, press firmly into a small lined dish, and chill at least 1 hour until firm.
Slice into small pieces - halawa (halva), a dense tahini-based sweet, is beloved across Jordan and the broader Levant.
description: Halva, a dense, sweet confection shared across the wider Middle East.

name: Jordanian Toum
steps: Blend the garlic with the lime juice, then slowly drizzle in the oil while blending until thick.
Serve alongside grilled meats.
steps_detailed: Blend the garlic with a little of the lime juice until very finely pureed.
With the blender running, slowly drizzle in the oil, alternating with the remaining lime juice, until it emulsifies into a thick, creamy sauce.
Serve alongside grilled chicken, lamb, or vegetables.
description: Toum, a garlic sauce whipped to an emulsified, mayonnaise-like consistency, a defining Levantine condiment.

name: Jordanian Yogurt Garlic Sauce

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Whisk the yogurt, garlic, and lime juice together.
Season with salt and serve alongside grilled meats or rice dishes.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

ingredients_full.note: cup, chopped, as a mint stand-in

steps_detailed: Whisk the yogurt, garlic, lime juice, and herbs together until smooth.
Season with salt and chill briefly before serving - a cooling counterpart to Jordan's richer meat dishes like mansaf and maqluba.
description: A simple yogurt-garlic sauce.
